wiki.extract:
The DG834 series are popular ADSL  modem router products from Netgear. The devices can be directly connected to a phone line and establish an ADSL broadband Internet connection to an internet service provider (ISP) and share it among several computers via 802.3 Ethernet and (on many models) 802.11b/g wireless data links.
These devices are popular among ISPs as they provide an all in one solution (ADSL modem/router/firewall/switch), which is ideal for home broadband users. The Netgear UK website claims the DG834G is the most popular wireless router in the UK and lists five awards that it has re

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy

topCves.vulnerabilityName: NETGEAR Multiple Devices Exposure of Sensitive Information Vulnerability
topCves.shortDescription: Multiple NETGEAR devices are prone to admin password disclosure via simple crafted requests to the web management server.
topCves.requiredAction: Apply updates per vendor instructions. If the affected device has since entered end-of-life, it should be disconnected if still in use.

topCves.vulnerabilityName: NETGEAR Multiple Devices Buffer Overflow Vulnerability
topCves.shortDescription: Multiple NETGEAR devices contain a buffer overflow vulnerability that allows for authentication bypass and remote code execution.

topCves.vulnerabilityName: NETGEAR DGN2200 Devices OS Command Injection Vulnerability
topCves.shortDescription: dnslookup.cgi on NETGEAR DGN2200 devices with firmware through 10.0.0.50 allows remote authenticated users to execute arbitrary OS commands
topCves.requiredAction: The impacted product is end-of-life and should be disconnected if still in use.

topCves.vulnerabilityName: NETGEAR Multiple WAP Devices Command Injection Vulnerability
topCves.shortDescription: Multiple NETGEAR Wireless Access Point devices allows unauthenticated web pages to pass form input directly to the command-line interface. Exploitation allows for arbitrary code execution.

topCves.vulnerabilityName: NETGEAR WNR2000v5 Router Buffer Overflow Vulnerability
topCves.shortDescription: The NETGEAR WNR2000v5 router contains a buffer overflow which can be exploited to achieve remote code execution.

highestSeverity.vulnerabilityName: NETGEAR Multiple Devices Exposure of Sensitive Information Vulnerability
NETGEAR Multiple Devices Buffer Overflow Vulnerability
